title: CRH: All Eyes On Good Financial Performance And Capital Allocation
text: CRH's adjusted organic revenue and EBITDA increased by +12% and +13%, respectively on a YoY basis in 1H 2022. The company achieved a good set of interim results, thanks to significant price hikes and its ability to manage energy costs.

title: CRH sees 10% rise in full-year profit after strong interims
text: CRH PLC (LSE:CRH) forecast a 10% rise in profit for the current year after announcing double-digit earnings growth for the first six months, reflecting price increases to offset inflation.  The US-focused construction materials supplier reported underlying profit (EBITDA) of US$2.2bn for the half-year to 30 June 2022, up from US$1.8bn in the same period last year, on sales up 14% at US$15bn.

title: CRH expected to lower outlook for second half
text: Shares in CRH PLC (LSE:CRH), the Dublin-headquartered, FTSE 100-listed but US-focused construction materials supplier, climbed strongly during the pandemic, party on expectations that President Biden's infrastructure bill would lead to strong demand. The shares have waned 18% in 2022 – relatively solid compared to some – helped by a share buyback and a positive first quarter update in April.
